Configure background pulling and which u: Configure background pulling and which update feed Tolaria follows. Stable only receives manually promoted releases, while Alpha follows every push to main.
Choose the app color mode used for Tolar: Choose the app color mode used for Tolaria chrome, editor surfaces, menus, and dialogs.
Automatically create conservative Git ch: Automatically create conservative Git checkpoints after editing pauses or when the app is no longer active.
AutoGit is unavailable until the current: AutoGit is unavailable until the current vault is Git-enabled. Initialize Git for this vault first.
When enabled, Tolaria will commit and pu: When enabled, Tolaria will commit and push saved local changes automatically after an idle pause or after the app becomes inactive.
Choose whether Tolaria automatically syn: Choose whether Tolaria automatically syncs untitled note filenames from the first H1 title.
Auto-rename untitled notes from first H1: Auto-rename untitled notes from first H1
When enabled, Tolaria renames untitled-n: When enabled, Tolaria renames untitled-note files as soon as the first H1 becomes a real title. Turn this off to keep the filename unchanged until you rename it manually from the breadcrumb bar.
Choose which CLI AI agent Tolaria uses i: Choose which CLI AI agent Tolaria uses in the AI panel and command palette.
Choose whether Tolaria shows the Inbox w: Choose whether Tolaria shows the Inbox workflow, plus how it moves through items while you triage them.
When enabled, an Inbox section shows uno: When enabled, an Inbox section shows unorganized notes, and a toggle lets you mark notes as organized.
When enabled, marking an Inbox note as o: When enabled, marking an Inbox note as organized immediately opens the next visible Inbox note.
Anonymous data helps us fix bugs and imp: Anonymous data helps us fix bugs and improve Tolaria. No vault content, note titles, or file paths are ever sent.
